descriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat Pembroke Lumber Kings 9-3 in Centennial Cup semifinal to advance to championship final.
descriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes advanced to the Centennial Cup junior-A hockey championship final by defeating the Ontario-champion Pembroke Lumber Kings 9-3 in a semifinal clash at Humboldt, Saskatchewan on May 9, 1987. The Sockeyes, who outshot Pembroke 44-32, scored goals from Dean Rutledge, Steve Jaques, Matt Hervey, Brian Kozak, Paul Rutherford, Jason Phillips, Tony Bobbitt, Mike McCormick, and Bill Hardy. They would face the undefeated Saskatchewan-champion Humboldt Broncos in the sudden-death final that night.
